items.supersedes: the codex-acp reader's conclusion that neither backend exposes constrained final-message output through the stock adapters (Codex 'must add it or keep client-side validation'), and the readiness GAP 'does the out-of-tree Codex binary honor TurnStartParams.outputSchema (field exists but codex-acp never sends it)?'
items.current: VERIFIED (operator, against real source): openai/codex @ tag rust-v0.142.4 (SHA d0fd966 — what codex-acp@1.0.2 pins via @openai/codex ^0.142.4) HONORS turn/start.outputSchema end-to-end into the OpenAI Responses API in STRICT mode. Chain: app-server-protocol v2/turn.rs:143 -> app-server turn_processor.rs:523 (final_output_json_schema = params.output_schema) -> core turn_context.rs:780 -> core turn.rs:1109 -> core client.rs:818-819 (&prompt.output_schema, output_schema_strict). The ONLY gap is that the stock codex-acp adapter never SENDS it. PATCH (codex-acp @5506fba, src/CodexAcpClient.ts, the runTurn({...}) call at ~lines 584-593): add one field `outputSchema: (request._meta as any)?.["agentprism/outputSchema"] ?? null` (sendPrompt already has request._meta in scope; TurnStartParams.outputSchema exists at :45 so it is type-clean; runTurn->turn/start forwards params verbatim). OUTPUT needs NO second patch: the constrained final assistant message returns over the normal session/update agent-message stream; CodexBackend reads the final text and JSON.parses it (cleaner than Claude, which needs emitRawSDKMessages). CodexBackend MUST normalize the engine's typebox/JSON Schema to OpenAI STRICT rules (every property required, additionalProperties:false, supported types/keywords only) BEFORE sending; keep the validate->re-prompt guard. DELIVERY: acp-agents spawns codex-acp, so point it at a vendored/forked/patch-package'd build carrying the patch.

findings.versionOrSha: README pins pi-dynamic-workflows @ 1b0291ab58c91037ea7b067875960530d52bedce; working copy on disk read verbatim (not git-checked, assumed to match)
findings.purpose: This single file IS workflow-engine. It is the vm-sandboxed script runtime that injects the agent/parallel/pipeline/phase/log/budget globals, enforces determinism for resume, runs the journal (hashAgentCall + longest-unchanged-prefix firstMiss), tracks token budget + per-phase sub-budgets, and meets the AgentRunner seam by calling exactly one method, options.agent.run(prompt, opts). De-Pi-ifying means replacing the WorkflowAgent default + the Pi-flavored imports while keeping every semantic in this file unchanged.

apiSurface.name: WorkflowRunOptions.agent (THE SEAM)
apiSurface.signature: agent?: Pick<WorkflowAgent, "run">
apiSurface.notes: Engine depends ONLY on the run method. README says used at :283 but :283 is the assignment; real call is :465.

apiSurface.name: agentRunner
apiSurface.signature: const agentRunner = options.agent ?? new WorkflowAgent(options)
apiSurface.notes: Default-constructs the Pi WorkflowAgent. Port: drop the `?? new WorkflowAgent` Pi default; require/inject an AgentRunner.

apiSurface.name: agentRunner.run(...)
apiSurface.signature: agentRunner.run(prompt, { label, schema, signal, instructions, model, tier, toolNames, disallowedToolNames, cwd, onModelResolved, onModelFallback, onUsage, onHistory } as any)
apiSurface.notes: Wrapped in withTimeout(...). Cast `as any` at :488 — engine already loosens the type at the seam.

apiSurface.name: WorkflowAgent.run
apiSurface.signature: run<TSchemaDef extends TSchema|undefined=undefined>(prompt: string, options: AgentRunOptions<TSchemaDef> = {}): Promise<AgentRunResult<TSchemaDef>>
apiSurface.notes: Return: AgentRunResult = TSchemaDef extends TSchema ? Static<TSchemaDef> : string (agent.ts:286-288).

apiSurface.name: AgentRunOptions
apiSurface.signature: { label?, schema?, tools?, instructions?, signal?, onUsage?, model?, tier?, onModelResolved?, onModelFallback?, onHistory?, cwd?, toolNames?, disallowedToolNames?, maxSchemaRetries? }
apiSurface.notes: The opts shape the seam must accept. Engine passes a subset (no tools/maxSchemaRetries).

apiSurface.name: AgentUsage
apiSurface.signature: { input:number; output:number; cacheRead:number; cacheWrite:number; total:number; cost:number }
apiSurface.notes: Delivered via onUsage callback, NOT the return value. total===0 => engine estimates.

apiSurface.name: runWorkflow
apiSurface.signature: runWorkflow<T>(script: string, options: WorkflowRunOptions = {}): Promise<WorkflowRunResult<T>>
apiSurface.notes: Entry point.

apiSurface.name: vm.createContext
apiSurface.signature: const context = vm.createContext({ agent, parallel, pipeline, workflow, verify, judgePanel, loopUntilDry, completenessCheck, retry, gate, checkpoint, log, phase, args, cwd, process, budget, console })
apiSurface.notes: Globals injected here. Deliberately does NOT inject host builtins (Object/Array/Math/Date come from the realm).

apiSurface.name: vm.Script .runInContext
apiSurface.notes: PRELUDE is concatenated ahead of body, then whole thing wrapped in an async IIFE.

apiSurface.name: DETERMINISM_PRELUDE
apiSurface.signature: joined JS source neutering Math.random/Date.now/Date()/new Date()
apiSurface.notes: Runtime enforcement inside the realm.

apiSurface.name: DETERMINISM_BLOCKLIST
apiSurface.signature: /\bDate\s*\.\s*now\b|\bMath\s*\.\s*random\b|\bnew\s+Date\s*\(\s*\)/
apiSurface.notes: Parse-time author hint; tested at :890 in parseWorkflowScript.

apiSurface.name: agent (global)
apiSurface.signature: const agent = async (prompt: string, agentOptions: AgentOptions = {}) => {...}
apiSurface.notes: Gates (abort/maxAgents/budget/phase-budget), assigns callIndex/callHash, resume replay, then limiter(()=> withTimeout(agentRunner.run(...))).

apiSurface.name: parallel (global)
apiSurface.signature: const parallel = async (thunks: Array<() => Promise<unknown>>) => Promise.all(...)
apiSurface.notes: Barrier (Promise.all). Recoverable thunk errors -> null in array; non-recoverable rethrow.

apiSurface.name: pipeline (global)
apiSurface.signature: const pipeline = async (items: unknown[], ...stages: Array<(prev, original, index) => unknown>) => Promise.all(...)
apiSurface.notes: Each item flows through its stages sequentially; no barrier BETWEEN stages; cross-item join via Promise.all at :588.

apiSurface.name: phase (global)
apiSurface.signature: const phase = (title: string, phaseOptions?: { budget?: number }) => {...}
apiSurface.notes: Sets currentPhase; declares per-phase sub-budget into state.phaseBudgets keyed by title.

apiSurface.name: log (global)
apiSurface.signature: const log = (message: string) => { state.logs.push(String(message)); logger.log(...) }
apiSurface.notes: Also backs console.log/info/warn/error in the realm.

apiSurface.name: budget (global)
apiSurface.signature: Object.freeze({ total: options.tokenBudget ?? null, spent: () => shared.spent, remaining: () => total==null ? Infinity : Math.max(0, total - shared.spent) })
apiSurface.notes: Soft gate. Read-only frozen object exposed to scripts.

apiSurface.name: checkpoint (global)
apiSurface.signature: const checkpoint = async (promptText: string, checkpointOptions: CheckpointOptions = {}) => {...}
apiSurface.notes: Journaled/replayable. Headless (no options.confirm): default branch at :827-829 takes checkpointOptions.default ?? true.

apiSurface.name: createLimiter
apiSurface.signature: function createLimiter(limit: number): <T>(fn: () => Promise<T>) => Promise<T>
apiSurface.notes: FIFO queue concurrency gate. Shared across nested workflow() via SharedRuntime.limiter.

apiSurface.name: hashAgentCall
apiSurface.signature: function hashAgentCall(prompt, model?, phase?, options: AgentOptions, agentDefKey: string|null): string
apiSurface.notes: sha256 of JSON{prompt,model,tier,phase,agentType,agentDef,schema}. Defines what a resume cache-miss is.

apiSurface.name: firstMiss / resume gate
apiSurface.signature: state.firstMiss (field workflow.ts:206); cached = resumeJournal.get(callIndex); replay iff hashMatches && !cachedEmptyOutput && callIndex < firstMiss
apiSurface.notes: Longest-unchanged-prefix: once any call misses, it and everything after run live.

apiSurface.name: resumeJournal
apiSurface.signature: resumeJournal?: Map<number, JournalEntry>
apiSurface.notes: JournalEntry = { index:number; hash:string; result:unknown } (workflow.ts:37-42). Written via options.onAgentJournal at :502 and :831.

apiSurface.name: per-phase sub-budget gate
apiSurface.signature: const pb = state.phaseBudgets.get(assignedPhase); phaseSpent = shared.spent - pb.startSpent; throw TOKEN_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED if >= pb.budget; warn once at 80%
apiSurface.notes: Declared at phase() :309-311; field RuntimeState.phaseBudgets :196.

apiSurface.name: checkpoint headless default
apiSurface.signature: if (options.confirm) reply = await confirm(...); else if (headless==="abort") throw; else reply = checkpointOptions.default ?? true
apiSurface.notes: README cites :819 (branch start); actual default literal at :828.

verbatimSnippets.label: DETERMINISM_PRELUDE (header + full array)
verbatimSnippets.code:
const DETERMINISM_PRELUDE = [
  '"use strict";',
  'Math.random = () => { throw new Error("Math.random() is unavailable in a workflow (it breaks resume); pass randomness via args or vary by index"); };',
  "{",
  "  const RealDate = Date;",
  '  const fail = (w) => { throw new Error(w + " is unavailable in a workflow (it breaks resume); pass a timestamp via args"); };',
  "  const SafeDate = function (...a) {",
  '    if (!new.target) fail("Date()");',
  '    if (a.length === 0) fail("new Date()");',
  "    return Reflect.construct(RealDate, a, SafeDate);",
  "  };",
  "  SafeDate.UTC = RealDate.UTC;",
  "  SafeDate.parse = RealDate.parse;",
  '  SafeDate.now = () => fail("Date.now()");',
  "  SafeDate.prototype = RealDate.prototype;",
  "  globalThis.Date = SafeDate;",
  "}",
].join("\n");

findings.portOrUsageNotes:
Pi coupling points in workflow.ts and what each becomes when lifted to workflow-engine:
(1) imports lines 6-7 `import type { AgentUsage } from \"./agent.js\"; import { WorkflowAgent, type WorkflowAgentOptions } from \"./agent.js\";` — agent.js is the ONLY thing pulling Pi (`@earendil-works/pi-ai`, `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`). Port: AgentUsage -> shared type in workflow-engine; WorkflowAgentOptions (extended by WorkflowRunOptions at :57) -> engine-local options type; `WorkflowAgent` type -> the AgentRunner interface (acp-agents provides the impl).
(2) line 59 `agent?: Pick<WorkflowAgent, \"run\">` -> `agent?: AgentRunner` where AgentRunner = { run(prompt: string, opts: AgentRunOptions): Promise<AgentRunResult> }. This is the entire seam.
(3) line 283 `const agentRunner = options.agent ?? new WorkflowAgent(options)` -> remove the `?? new WorkflowAgent(options)` Pi default; make options.agent required, or default to an injected runner factory. This is the single line that hard-binds the engine to the Pi agent.
(4) line 465 call site passes Pi-flavored opts; the `as any` at :488 already loosens the type. Keep all 14 fields but re-source their providers: schema (typebox TSchema, import at :5), model/tier (Pi `provider/modelId` strings via resolveModelForPhase/parseModelRoutingFromMeta, imports :19), toolNames/disallowedToolNames (Pi coding-tools allow/denylist from agentDef), onModelResolved/onModelFallback/onUsage(AgentUsage)/onHistory(AgentHistoryEntry, import :8). In ACP these become runner-interpreted: model spec string handed to the ACP agent; tool allow/deny lists handed to ACP permission config; onUsage/onHistory remain progress callbacks.
(5) line 16 config.js caps (MAX_CONCURRENCY=16, MAX_AGENTS_PER_RUN=1000, MAX_AGENT_RETRIES=3, DEFAULT_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS=null) — pure constants, lift as-is.
(6) lines 9-15 agent-registry.js + line 372 resolveAgentType/loadAgentRegistry scan `.pi/agents` & `~/.pi/agents` — Pi PATH convention only; de-Pi to a configurable agents dir. agentDefinitionKey(agentDef) (:392) feeds hashAgentCall, so the registry stays but its source dir is parameterized.
(7) line 19 model-routing.js (parseModelRoutingFromMeta/resolveModelForPhase) operate on plain strings; not Pi-API-bound — lift, semantics intact.
(8) line 20 worktree.js — git only, not Pi.
(9) line 285 `globalThis.navigator?.hardwareConcurrency` — Node/browser, keep.
(10) line 5 `import type { TSchema } from \"typebox\"` — schema type threaded into run() opts and JSON.stringify'd into hashAgentCall (:1062); keep typebox or abstract at the seam (open question). Everything in workflow.ts NOT touching agent.js/agent-registry/model-routing is pure and lifts verbatim: the vm realm (:835/:866), DETERMINISM_PRELUDE (:227)/blocklist (:212/:890), parallel (:555)/pipeline (:579)/createLimiter (:1013), journal hashAgentCall (:1045)+firstMiss (:407), budget (:315)+phase sub-budgets (:303), checkpoint (:798).
findings.seamFacts:
run(prompt, opts) -> result contract, fully pinned from workflow.ts + agent.ts:
- The engine references the runner ONLY as Pick<WorkflowAgent,\"run\"> (workflow.ts:59). One method. Called EXACTLY ONCE, at workflow.ts:465, inside the limiter thunk inside agent(), wrapped by withTimeout(promise, timeout, label) (:464). (README's \":283 used at\" is wrong — :283 is the assignment; the call is :465.)
- Signature (agent.ts:332-335): run<TSchemaDef extends TSchema|undefined=undefined>(prompt: string, options: AgentRunOptions<TSchemaDef> = {}): Promise<AgentRunResult<TSchemaDef>>.
- Return shape (agent.ts:286-288): AgentRunResult = TSchemaDef extends TSchema ? Static<TSchemaDef> : string. So: schema present => resolved structured OBJECT; no schema => the assistant's text STRING.
- opts the engine actually passes (workflow.ts:465-488), cast `as any`: { label:string; schema?:TSchema; signal?:AbortSignal; instructions?:string (from buildAgentInstructions); model?:string; tier?:string; toolNames?:string[]; disallowedToolNames?:string[]; cwd?:string; onModelResolved:(id:string)=>void; onModelFallback:(spec:string)=>void; onUsage:(u:AgentUsage)=>void; onHistory:(h:AgentHistoryEntry[])=>void }. (Engine does NOT pass tools/maxSchemaRetries though AgentRunOptions defines them.)
- USAGE flows back via the onUsage callback, NOT the return value (workflow.ts:482-484 sets `usage`; recordTokens at :443-455 reads usage.{total,input,output,cost,cacheRead,cacheWrite}). If onUsage never fires or usage.total===0, engine falls back to estimateTokens(result)+estimateTokens(prompt) (:444). => An AgentRunner SHOULD call onUsage for real accounting; otherwise estimate.
- RESULT semantics the engine enforces: isEmptyTextAgentResult(result, schema) (:494, helper :1085) — only for NO-schema calls, a whitespace/empty string => throws AGENT_EMPTY_OUTPUT (recoverable). The result is journaled verbatim via onAgentJournal({index,hash,result}) (:502) and replayed on resume (:413). So the runner's result must be JSON-serializable and stable for a fixed identity.
- ERROR contract: run() should throw; engine wraps via wrapError(error,{agentLabel}) (:515) into a WorkflowError carrying .recoverable and .code. recoverable -> retried up to retries (retryAttempts+1 attempts, :421-422) then returns null; non-recoverable -> rethrown (halts run, incl. inside parallel/pipeline :571/:600). Provider/quota limits are surfaced as recoverable WorkflowError (agent.ts throwIfProviderLimit) — the ACP runner must map equivalent failures.
- TIMEOUT + ABORT are the ENGINE's job, not the runner's: withTimeout (:464, fn :1106-1128) races a setTimeout that throws AGENT_TIMEOUT (recoverable); engine passes options.signal and calls throwIfAborted() before/after. The runner SHOULD honor opts.signal (agent.ts:399-404 wires it to session.abort) but need not implement its own timeout.
- DETERMINISM/RESUME identity (hashAgentCall :1045): sha256(JSON{prompt, model, tier, phase, agentType, agentDef(=agentDefinitionKey), schema}). cwd (worktree), label, instructions are NOT in the hash (instructions are derived from phase/agentType/agentDef/isolation which ARE hashed). => Same identity tuple MUST yield a replayable result; the runner must be deterministic w.r.t. that tuple (the PRELUDE enforces no Date.now/Math.random inside the script, but the runner/LLM nondeterminism is masked by journaling, not by the runner).
- callIndex = state.callSeq++ (:391) is assigned at LEXICAL call time BEFORE the limiter, so parallel()/pipeline() fan-out ordering is reproducible regardless of completion order — the runner must not depend on invocation order for its identity.
- checkpoint() (:798) is NOT a runner call: it spends no tokens, is gated on agentCount+abort (not budget), journals/replays by callIndex; headless (no options.confirm) takes checkpointOptions.default ?? true (:828). The AgentRunner interface does NOT need a checkpoint method; human-in-the-loop is the engine's options.confirm callback.
discrepancies: README.md:114-115 says agentRunner.run is 'used at :283' — INCORRECT. workflow.ts:283 is the ASSIGNMENT `const agentRunner = options.agent ?? new WorkflowAgent(options);`. The actual (and ONLY) `.run(...)` call site is workflow.ts:465 (inside the limiter thunk in agent(), wrapped by withTimeout). Phase 1 should treat :465 as the seam call, :283 as the seam binding.

README.md:215 cites checkpoint headless default at src/workflow.ts:819 — :819 is the start of the `if (options.confirm)` branch; the actual headless-default literal is workflow.ts:828 `reply = checkpointOptions.default ?? true;`. checkpoint() is defined at :798. Minor.

README.md:135 labels pipeline ':579, no barrier' — pipeline DOES join all items with Promise.all at workflow.ts:588 (a cross-item barrier). 'no barrier' is accurate only in the inter-STAGE sense: each item flows through its stages sequentially with no barrier between stages. Clarify so a porter doesn't drop the Promise.all.

All other cited lines are EXACT, no drift: :59 (seam field), :212 (blocklist const), :227 (DETERMINISM_PRELUDE), :303 (phase/sub-budget decl), :315 (budget object), :407 (firstMiss/resume gate), :555 (parallel), :835 (vm.createContext), :866 (vm.Script), :890 (blocklist enforcement), :1013 (createLimiter), :1045 (hashAgentCall).
openQuestions: Schema type at the seam: keep typebox (TSchema/Static, imported workflow.ts:5, agent.ts:14) or abstract to a generic schema interface? It is threaded into run() opts AND JSON.stringify'd into the resume hash (workflow.ts:1062), so changing it changes journal compatibility.

AgentUsage and AgentHistoryEntry are passed back through run() callbacks (onUsage/onHistory). Where do these live in the 3-module split — a shared-types module imported by both workflow-engine and acp-agents? They currently live in agent.ts (Pi module).

Model routing semantics: model/tier are plain `provider/modelId` strings resolved by resolveModelForPhase/parseModelRoutingFromMeta (Pi-flavored but string-only). In the ACP world, does the AgentRunner own model resolution (interpreting the string) or does the engine keep routing and hand a concrete spec? The onModelResolved/onModelFallback callbacks imply the RUNNER resolves and reports back.

agentType registry: loadAgentRegistry scans `.pi/agents`/`~/.pi/agents` (workflow.ts:260,372). The agents dir must be parameterized for the Pi-independent engine; agentDefinitionKey feeds the resume hash so its key format must stay stable across the port.

Should options.agent become REQUIRED (drop `?? new WorkflowAgent(options)` at :283 entirely) given workflow-engine must not depend on acp-agents? README says the engine depends on an injected AgentRunner 'not on acp-agents', which argues for required + no default.

Run lifecycle/persistence/resume. WorkflowManager extends EventEmitter: startInBackground, runSync (the path mcp-server keeps), executeRun, pause/resume/stop, listRuns (session-filtered). ManagedRun + ExecOptions (resumeJournal, maxAgents, tokenBudget, concurrency, agentRetries, confirm). Calls runWorkflow with onAgentJournal/onAgentStart/onAgentEnd/onTokenUsage wiring.
Phase->model routing. ModelRoute{phasePattern,model,useRegex?}, ModelRoutingConfig{defaultModel?,routes}. resolveModelForPhase(phase,config), parseModelRoutingFromMeta(phases,defaultModel).

tier small/medium/big -> single model spec string. ModelTierConfig{tiers:Record<string,string>}. resolveTierModel(tier,config)->config.tiers[tier]. load/save at ~/.pi/workflows/model-tiers.json. sortedTierNames ranks small<medium<big.

DEMOTED injected tool. createStructuredOutputTool({schema,capture,name='structured_output'}) -> terminating ToolDefinition; execute() sets capture.value=params, capture.called=true, returns terminate:true. StructuredOutputCapture{value,called}.

SEAM definition + only call site. WorkflowRunOptions.agent?: Pick<WorkflowAgent,'run'> (:59). agentRunner resolved (:283). agentRunner.run(prompt,opts) invoked (:465-491) — the exact opts that constrain AgentRunner.

apiSurface.name: WorkflowAgent.run
apiSurface.signature: async run<TSchemaDef extends TSchema | undefined = undefined>(prompt: string, options: AgentRunOptions<TSchemaDef> = {}): Promise<AgentRunResult<TSchemaDef>>
apiSurface.notes: THE LEAF METHOD acp-agents must reproduce. End-to-end: build per-cwd coding tools (createCodingTools(runCwd)), applyToolPolicy(toolNames/disallowedToolNames) BEFORE pushing structured_output, resolveAgentModelSpec -> resolveModel via ModelRegistry, createAgentSession({cwd,agentDir,SessionManager.inMemory(),SettingsManager.create,customTools,model}), wire signal->session.abort & subscribe->onHistory, session.prompt(buildPrompt), throwIfProviderLimit, schema branch -> resolveStructuredOutput else lastAssistantText (empty => AGENT_EMPTY_OUTPUT recoverable). finally: emitHistory, getSessionStats()->onUsage, session.dispose(). Returns RAW value string|Static<schema>.

apiSurface.name: AgentRunOptions
apiSurface.signature: interface AgentRunOptions<TSchemaDef extends TSchema|undefined=undefined> { label?: string; schema?: TSchemaDef; tools?: ToolDefinition[]; instructions?: string; signal?: AbortSignal; onUsage?: (usage: AgentUsage)=>void; model?: string; tier?: string; onModelResolved?: (modelId: string)=>void; onModelFallback?: (requestedSpec: string)=>void; onHistory?: (history: AgentHistoryEntry[])=>void; cwd?: string; toolNames?: string[]; disallowedToolNames?: string[]; maxSchemaRetries?: number }
apiSurface.notes: THE opts side of the AgentRunner seam — exactly the fields workflow.ts:465 passes. cwd/model/tier/schema/signal flow IN here; usage/model-id/history flow OUT via the on* callbacks (NOT via the return value). maxSchemaRetries default 2.

apiSurface.name: AgentRunResult
apiSurface.signature: type AgentRunResult<TSchemaDef extends TSchema|undefined> = TSchemaDef extends TSchema ? Static<TSchemaDef> : string
apiSurface.notes: THE result side of the seam. Schema => the parsed/validated object (Static<schema>); no schema => assistant text string. There is NO wrapper object — usage is delivered separately via onUsage.

apiSurface.name: AgentUsage
apiSurface.signature: interface AgentUsage { input: number; output: number; cacheRead: number; cacheWrite: number; total: number; cost: number }
apiSurface.notes: Payload of onUsage callback, read from session.getSessionStats() right before dispose (success AND error paths). total===0 means provider reported no usage (engine then estimates via chars/4). Maps onto ACP usage_update / PromptResponse.usage {inputTokens,outputTokens,cachedReadTokens,cachedWriteTokens,totalTokens} + cost (Claude only).

apiSurface.name: resolveStructuredOutput
apiSurface.signature: async function resolveStructuredOutput<T>(session: StructuredSession, capture: StructuredOutputCapture<T>, schema: TSchema, options: { maxSchemaRetries?: number; signal?: AbortSignal; label?: string }, lastText: (messages: unknown[])=>string): Promise<T>
apiSurface.notes: KEPT VERBATIM as client-side schema guard in acp-agents (README §6.5). README cites :113 — VERIFIED EXACT. Ladder: capture.called -> value; else re-prompt up to maxSchemaRetries (restricting tools to ['structured_output']); else extractValidated from prose; else throwIfProviderLimit then throw SCHEMA_NONCOMPLIANCE (recoverable:false). Depends on StructuredSession (prompt/setActiveToolsByName?/messages) + injected lastText — both replaceable for ACP.

apiSurface.name: extractValidated
apiSurface.signature: function extractValidated<T>(text: string, schema: TSchema): T | undefined
apiSurface.notes: KEPT VERBATIM. README cites :47 — VERIFIED EXACT. findJsonBlock(text) (fenced ```json or first balanced {}/[]) -> JSON.parse -> typebox Convert(schema,parsed) -> Check(schema,...). Never fabricates: returns undefined unless it genuinely validates. Pure (typebox only) — lifts cleanly into acp-agents.

apiSurface.name: StructuredSession
apiSurface.signature: interface StructuredSession { prompt(text: string): Promise<void>; setActiveToolsByName?(names: string[]): void; messages: unknown[] }
apiSurface.notes: Minimal session surface resolveStructuredOutput needs. acp-agents implements this over an ACP session (prompt = session/prompt; messages = drained assistant text; setActiveToolsByName optional).

apiSurface.name: resolveAgentModelSpec
apiSurface.signature: function resolveAgentModelSpec(options: { model?: string; tier?: string }, mainModel: string|undefined, loadConfig: ()=>ModelTierConfig|null = loadModelTierConfig): string | undefined
apiSurface.notes: Model precedence: options.model > options.tier (resolveTierModel ?? mainModel) > default 'medium' tier when a config exists > undefined (session default). Pure-ish (loadConfig injectable). Resolution TARGET (a model spec string) becomes an ACP session config option per README §5.4.

apiSurface.name: createWorktree
apiSurface.signature: async function createWorktree(baseCwd: string, name: string): Promise<Worktree>
apiSurface.notes: git -C baseCwd rev-parse --show-toplevel -> repoRoot; path = repoRoot/.pi/worktrees/<slug(name)>; branch = pi/wf/<slug>; git -C repoRoot worktree add -b <branch> <path> HEAD. Returns {isolated:true,cwd:path,branch,repoRoot} or no-op {isolated:false,cwd:baseCwd,reason}. name MUST be deterministic (engine passes `${runId}-${callIndex}-${label}`). slug: lowercase, [^a-z0-9]->-, trim -, slice(0,32)||'agent'.

apiSurface.name: removeWorktree
apiSurface.signature: async function removeWorktree(wt: Worktree): Promise<void>
apiSurface.notes: No-op when !wt.isolated||!wt.repoRoot. git worktree remove --force <cwd> then git branch -D <branch>. Best-effort (both try/catch). Results NOT auto-merged.

apiSurface.name: Worktree
apiSurface.signature: interface Worktree { isolated: boolean; cwd: string; branch?: string; repoRoot?: string; reason?: string }
apiSurface.notes: cwd is what flows into run({cwd}). In the engine: runCwd = worktree?.isolated ? worktree.cwd : undefined (workflow.ts:437). Maps directly to ACP session/new({cwd}) per README §5.3.

apiSurface.name: resolveTierModel
apiSurface.signature: function resolveTierModel(tier: string, config: ModelTierConfig): string | undefined
apiSurface.notes: Just config.tiers[tier]. tier->target mapping shape: Record<tierName,string> where string is 'provider/modelId' or bare 'modelId'.

apiSurface.name: ModelTierConfig
apiSurface.signature: interface ModelTierConfig { tiers: Record<string, string> }
apiSurface.notes: A tier is a named slot holding exactly ONE model spec string. Default config: small/medium/big all == current model. Loaded from ~/.pi/workflows/model-tiers.json (MODEL_TIERS_FILE).

apiSurface.name: resolveModelForPhase
apiSurface.signature: function resolveModelForPhase(phase: string|undefined, config: ModelRoutingConfig): string | undefined
apiSurface.notes: Per-phase model override; useRegex (case-insensitive) or exact case-sensitive match; falls back to config.defaultModel. ModelRoute{phasePattern,model,useRegex?}. parseModelRoutingFromMeta builds it from meta.phases[].model + meta.model.

apiSurface.name: createStructuredOutputTool
apiSurface.signature: function createStructuredOutputTool<TSchemaDef extends TSchema>({ schema, capture, name='structured_output' }: StructuredOutputToolOptions<TSchemaDef>): ToolDefinition<TSchemaDef, Static<TSchemaDef>>
apiSurface.notes: DEMOTED. Pi-coding-agent defineTool; Pi validates params vs schema before execute(); execute sets capture.value=params, capture.called=true, returns {content,details:params,terminate:true}. In acp-agents the native backend output schema replaces this as the primary path; the capture pattern is the fallback signal resolveStructuredOutput reads.

apiSurface.name: StructuredOutputCapture
apiSurface.signature: interface StructuredOutputCapture<T=unknown> { value: T | undefined; called: boolean }
apiSurface.notes: Mutable capture cell shared between the tool and resolveStructuredOutput. acp-agents fills capture from the backend native-structured result (Claude structured_output off _claude/sdkMessage; Codex structuredContent) instead of a tool execute().

apiSurface.name: createRunPersistence
apiSurface.signature: function createRunPersistence(cwd: string, fsOverride?: Partial<FsLayer>): RunPersistence
apiSurface.notes: Disk journal + leases, lifted unchanged into workflow-engine. RunPersistence = {save,load,list,delete,acquireRunLease,releaseRunLease,getRunsDir}. Atomic write (tmp+rename) + .bak; lockfile via writeFileSync flag 'wx' with pid liveness (process.kill(pid,0)). FsLayer injectable for tests.

apiSurface.name: PersistedRunState
apiSurface.signature: interface PersistedRunState { runId; workflowName; script; args?; sessionId?; status: RunStatus; pauseReason?; resetHint?; phases: string[]; currentPhase?; agents: PersistedAgentState[]; logs: string[]; result?; startedAt; updatedAt; completedAt?; durationMs?; tokenUsage?{input,output,total,cost?,cacheRead?,cacheWrite?}; journal?: Array<{index:number;hash:string;result:unknown}> }
apiSurface.notes: The on-disk resume record. journal[] = {index,hash,result} is the longest-unchanged-prefix replay cache (keyed by deterministic call index). RunStatus = 'pending'|'running'|'paused'|'completed'|'failed'|'aborted' (:11).

apiSurface.name: WorkflowManager.runSync
apiSurface.signature: async runSync(script: string, args?: unknown, exec: ExecOptions = {}): Promise<WorkflowRunResult>
apiSurface.notes: THE path mcp-server keeps (README §4: drop startInBackground, make this default). Acquires lease, persists initial state, calls executeRun -> runWorkflow with full on* event wiring. exec.onProgress drives inline display (maps to MCP notifications/progress). exec also carries resumeJournal (resume), maxAgents, tokenBudget, concurrency, agentRetries, confirm (checkpoint).

apiSurface.name: WorkflowManager.resume
apiSurface.signature: async resume(runId: string): Promise<boolean>
apiSurface.notes: Loads persisted run, builds resumeJournal = Map(journal[].index -> entry), re-runs via executeRun. Refuses 'running'/'aborted'/'completed'. README §4 exposes this as the resumeFromRunId MCP tool param.

apiSurface.name: WorkflowRunOptions.agent (the seam type)
apiSurface.signature: agent?: Pick<WorkflowAgent, "run">
apiSurface.notes: THE AgentRunner SEAM. README cites workflow.ts:59 — VERIFIED EXACT. The engine injects this; resolved at :283 (const agentRunner = options.agent ?? new WorkflowAgent(options)) and the ONLY call is at :465. Phase 1 freezes AgentRunner = Pick<WorkflowAgent,'run'> = { run(prompt, AgentRunOptions): Promise<AgentRunResult> }.

findings.portOrUsageNotes:
ENGINE — exact Pi-coupling points in agent.ts that acp-agents must replace (everything else lifts unchanged):
(1) createAgentSession({cwd,agentDir,sessionManager,settingsManager,customTools,model}) [agent.ts:372] -> ACP session/new({cwd, mcpServers}) + session/prompt. cwd flows IN unchanged (README §5.3). 
(2) ModelRegistry/AuthStorage.create + resolveModel(spec) [agent.ts:307-330] -> ACP model selection via session config option session/set_config_option {configId:'model'} (README §5.4); resolveAgentModelSpec precedence (model>tier>medium-default) stays as pure pre-resolution producing the spec string fed to the config option. Cross-PROVIDER routing = choosing which ACP server (claude-agent-acp vs codex-acp); within-provider = the config option.
(3) createCodingTools(runCwd) + applyToolPolicy(toolNames/disallowedToolNames) [agent.ts:340-347] -> ACP has NO direct tool-handoff; tools come from the agent's own built-ins + mcpServers on session/new (README §5.8). toolNames/disallowedToolNames allow/deny must be enforced at the session/request_permission boundary (README §5.5) or via permission modes — NOT by handing a tool list.
(4) createStructuredOutputTool injection [agent.ts:349-351] -> DROP as primary; use native backend output schema (Claude: _meta.claudeCode.options.outputFormat {type:'json_schema',schema} at session/new + emitRawSDKMessages:true to READ structured_output off the _claude/sdkMessage result notification; Codex: outputSchema on TurnStartParams/SendUserTurnParams). 
(5) session.getSessionStats()->{tokens,cost} [agent.ts:443] -> ACP usage_update notifications + PromptResponse.usage {inputTokens,outputTokens,cachedReadTokens,cachedWriteTokens,totalTokens} + cost (Claude only; Codex no $). Must still emit AgentUsage on BOTH success and error paths before closing the session, total===0 => engine estimates.
(6) session.abort() on signal [agent.ts:401] -> session/cancel {sessionId} notification -> prompt resolves stopReason:'cancelled' (README §5.7). 
(7) session.subscribe(...)->onHistory [agent.ts:406] -> drain session/update agent_message_chunk/tool_call stream into AgentHistoryEntry[]. 
(8) throwIfProviderLimit reads pi assistant message stopReason==='error'+errorMessage [agent.ts:71-97]; over ACP the analogous signal is stopReason (max_tokens etc.) + the Claude SDKResultError subtype error_max_structured_output_retries (README §6.2b) — map provider quota to a recoverable PROVIDER_USAGE_LIMIT checkpoint.
KEEP VERBATIM in acp-agents: extractValidated [agent.ts:47] (typebox-only, pure) + resolveStructuredOutput [agent.ts:113] (needs a StructuredSession{prompt,setActiveToolsByName?,messages} adapter over the ACP session + an injected lastText that returns the drained assistant text; capture is filled from the native structured result instead of a tool execute()). Ladder per README §6.5: native constraint -> client-side validate(extractValidated/Check) -> re-prompt on failure.
SDK/CLIENT — the engine drives the leaf ONLY through run(prompt, AgentRunOptions)->Promise<AgentRunResult>; it never touches sessions. So AgentRunner is satisfiable by any object with that one method. worktree.ts/run-persistence.ts/workflow-manager.ts/model-routing.ts/model-tier-config.ts/structured-output.ts(capture types) lift with ZERO Pi imports — agent.ts is the only file importing @earendil-works/pi-* among this surface (worktree/run-persistence/model-routing/model-tier-config import only node + local; structured-output imports defineTool from @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent which the demotion removes).
findings.seamFacts:
AGENTRUNNER INTERFACE (freeze this in Phase 1): AgentRunner = Pick<WorkflowAgent,'run'> i.e. { run<S extends TSchema|undefined=undefined>(prompt: string, options: AgentRunOptions<S>): Promise<AgentRunResult<S>> } [workflow.ts:59, agent.ts:332]. 
RESULT SHAPE (what AgentResult must contain): the leaf returns the RAW value only — string when no schema, Static<schema> (the parsed+validated object) when a schema is given [AgentRunResult, agent.ts:286-288]. There is NO {result,usage} wrapper. The engine consumes the return value directly (checks isEmptyTextAgentResult, journals it as journal[].result, returns it to the script) [workflow.ts:494,502,511]. 
USAGE is OUT-OF-BAND via callback: onUsage(AgentUsage{input,output,cacheRead,cacheWrite,total,cost}) fired once, on BOTH success and error paths, read right before session disposal; total===0 means provider reported nothing and the engine falls back to a chars/4 estimate [agent.ts:225-232, 441-455; workflow.ts:443-455]. 
WHAT FLOWS IN (opts), exactly as the engine passes them [workflow.ts:465-488]: label, schema (TSchema|undefined), signal (AbortSignal), instructions (string), model (resolved spec string|undefined), tier (string|undefined), toolNames (string[]|undefined allowlist), disallowedToolNames (string[]|undefined denylist), cwd (string|undefined — the worktree path or undefined for shared tree), plus callbacks onModelResolved(id), onModelFallback(spec), onUsage(u), onHistory(history). maxSchemaRetries (default 2) is in AgentRunOptions but the engine does NOT pass it at this call site (leaf default applies). 
CWD FLOW: engine computes runCwd = worktree?.isolated ? worktree.cwd : undefined [workflow.ts:437] from createWorktree(baseCwd, `${runId}-${callIndex}-${label}`) (deterministic name) and passes it as opts.cwd; the leaf binds tools/session to that cwd. 
MODEL/TIER FLOW: engine resolves modelSpec via precedence (explicit opts.model ?? agentDef.model ?? (tier? undefined : resolveModelForPhase)) and passes BOTH model:modelSpec and tier:agentOptions.tier; the leaf's resolveAgentModelSpec does the final model>tier>medium-default resolution [agent.ts:173, workflow.ts:381-383]. 
SCHEMA FLOW: opts.schema (a TSchema) drives the structured path; on success the leaf returns Static<schema>; on non-compliance it throws WorkflowError SCHEMA_NONCOMPLIANCE (recoverable:false). 
SIGNAL FLOW: opts.signal aborts the run (leaf wires it to session.abort / ACP session/cancel); aborted => throws 'Subagent was aborted'. 
ERROR CONTRACT the engine relies on: WorkflowError with code+recoverable+agentLabel(+resetHint). Codes the leaf raises: AGENT_EMPTY_OUTPUT (recoverable:true — empty assistant text), SCHEMA_NONCOMPLIANCE (recoverable:false), PROVIDER_USAGE_LIMIT (recoverable:false, carries resetHint -> engine checkpoints run as 'paused' not 'failed') [agent.ts:87-97,150-154,426-429; workflow-manager.ts:386-400]. recoverable:true triggers engine retry up to agentRetries; recoverable:false surfaces/pauses. 
JOURNAL/RESUME contract (workflow-engine): each successful run is journaled as {index:callIndex, hash:hashAgentCall(...), result} [workflow.ts:502]; resume replays the longest unchanged prefix (callIndex<firstMiss & hash matches & not empty) and re-runs the rest live; persisted as PersistedRunState.journal []. The AgentRunner must therefore be deterministic-friendly: same (prompt,model,phase,opts) must be safe to replay/skip.
discrepancies: agent.ts size: README §2/README header say agent.ts is ~540 lines; actual file is 496 lines (ends at line 496). Minor drift, content matches.

README 'used at :283' for the AgentRunner seam (line 114/497): workflow.ts:283 is where agentRunner is RESOLVED (`const agentRunner = options.agent ?? new WorkflowAgent(options);`), NOT where .run() is called. The actual ONLY .run() invocation is workflow.ts:465. The :59 citation for `agent?: Pick<WorkflowAgent,"run">` is EXACT.

README agent.ts citations VERIFIED EXACT: resolveStructuredOutput at :113 (exact — function decl line), extractValidated at :47 (exact — function decl line). No drift on the two KEPT-verbatim functions.

README §5.4 says codex switches model via `session/setConfigOption` while §5.4 also/elsewhere and §5.1 the ACP-standard method is `session/set_config_option` (snake_case). Both spellings appear in the README; not verified against the on-disk ACP SDK in this task (those packages are not in this surface) — flag for Phase-1 ACP verification.

structured-output.ts is the ONLY non-agent.ts support file still importing a Pi package (defineTool from @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent, structured-output.ts:1). README marks the injected tool DEMOTED but the file as-written still has that import; the demotion must drop it. worktree.ts/run-persistence.ts/model-routing.ts/model-tier-config.ts import only node:* + local modules and lift cleanly. NOTE: model-tier-config.ts imports listAvailableModelSpecs from ./agent.js (model-tier-config.ts:18) which transitively pulls Pi via agent.ts — that import must be cut/redirected when agent.ts is replaced.

README §3 lists typebox as 'today' schema lib; confirmed: agent.ts/structured-output.ts use `typebox` (import { Static, TSchema } from "typebox"; Check/Convert from "typebox/value"). extractValidated's Convert+Check are typebox-specific — a zod port would need rewriting these two calls.
openQuestions: AgentRunResult carries usage only via the onUsage callback, never in the return value. Does Phase-1's frozen AgentRunner keep usage out-of-band (callback) or fold it into a richer result object? The engine TODAY depends on the callback form (workflow.ts:482 sets a closure var); changing to a wrapper means touching the engine call site — decide before freezing.

resolveStructuredOutput depends on a StructuredSession with a re-promptable `prompt()` and a mutable `capture`. Over ACP, re-prompting = another session/prompt turn and `capture` is filled from the native structured-output channel (Claude _claude/sdkMessage result; Codex structuredContent). Confirm the acp-agents adapter can re-prompt within the same session and re-read the native result on the retry turn (Claude outputFormat is SESSION-scoped per README §6.2 — does a re-prompt re-validate against the same schema?).

setActiveToolsByName(['structured_output']) is a no-op once the injected tool is dropped. With native backends the equivalent 'force the schema' nudge is just the re-prompt text; verify that's sufficient or whether a per-turn outputSchema (Codex) must be re-sent on the retry.

maxSchemaRetries is in AgentRunOptions (default 2) but the engine does NOT pass it at workflow.ts:465. Should the frozen interface expose it to the engine/MCP tool, or keep it a leaf-internal default?

toolNames/disallowedToolNames are coding-tool name allow/deny lists meaningful in Pi's createCodingTools world. Over ACP the agent owns its tools and the only lever is session/request_permission auto-response (README §5.5) — confirm Phase-1 keeps these fields in AgentRunOptions and maps them to permission decisions, or renames them.

model-tier-config.ts:18 imports listAvailableModelSpecs from agent.ts (Pi). When agent.ts is replaced, where does the 'available models' list come from for buildDefaultTierConfig — the ACP server's advertised model catalog (configOptions.options[]) per README §5.4? Confirm the substitution source.

findings.purpose: This is the wire library our orchestrator's acp-agents module uses to SPEAK ACP AS A CLIENT to a spawned coding-agent subprocess over stdio. It is the concrete transport+RPC layer that sits underneath the AgentRunner seam: AgentRunner.run(prompt,opts) is implemented by (1) spawning the agent process, (2) ndJsonStream over its stdio, (3) client().connectWith -> initialize -> buildSession(cwd, mcpServers) -> session.prompt(prompt) pumping session/update until {kind:"stop"} yields PromptResponse{stopReason,usage?}. Permissions, file-system, and terminal requests FROM the agent are answered by client-side handlers we register. Structured output is NOT a core ACP feature, so the seam must carry schema/structuredContent out-of-band (via _meta, an MCP workflow tool, or post-hoc parse of the final text/PromptResponse).

apiSurface.name: ClientContext.request
apiSurface.signature: request<Method extends AgentRequestMethod>(method, params: AgentRequestParamsByMethod[Method], options?: SendRequestOptions): Promise<AgentRequestResponsesByMethod[Method]>
apiSurface.notes: Typed agent calls. Used for initialize (params InitializeRequest -> InitializeResponse) and any of session/new, session/prompt, session/load, session/set_config_option, authenticate, logout, session/list|delete|resume|close, etc. Untyped overload at :180.

apiSurface.name: ClientContext.notify
apiSurface.signature: notify<Method extends AgentNotificationMethod>(method, params: AgentNotificationParamsByMethod[Method]): Promise<void>
apiSurface.notes: Send agent notifications. 'session/cancel' (CancelNotification{sessionId}) is here -> AGENT_METHODS.session_cancel = 'session/cancel'.

apiSurface.name: ClientContext.buildSession
apiSurface.signature: buildSession(cwd: string): SessionBuilder  |  buildSession(request: NewSessionRequest): SessionBuilder
apiSurface.notes: Overloads: pass a cwd string for the simple case, or a full NewSessionRequest when you need mcpServers/_meta/additionalDirectories. Builder issues session/new under the hood.

apiSurface.name: SessionBuilder.withMcpServer / withAdditionalDirectories / toRequest / start / withSession
apiSurface.signature: withMcpServer(mcpServer: McpServer): this; withAdditionalDirectories(dirs: string[]): this; toRequest(): NewSessionRequest; start(options?: SendRequestOptions): Promise<ActiveSession>; withSession<T>(op:(s:ActiveSession)=>MaybePromise<T>):Promise<T>
apiSurface.notes: There is NO withConfigOption — configOptions are NOT part of session/new request. start()/withSession() send session/new and wire session/update routing for the returned sessionId.

apiSurface.name: ActiveSession.prompt
apiSurface.signature: prompt(prompt: string | ContentBlock | Array<ContentBlock>, options?: SendRequestOptions): Promise<PromptResponse>
apiSurface.notes: Convenience wrapper over session/prompt: string -> one text ContentBlock. Resolves with final PromptResponse{stopReason,usage?}; same completion is ALSO queued as a {kind:'stop'} message for nextUpdate(). options.cancellationSignal aborts this request.

apiSurface.name: ActiveSession.nextUpdate
apiSurface.signature: nextUpdate(): Promise<ActiveSessionMessage>
apiSurface.notes: Pump. Returns {kind:'session_update', notification:SessionNotification, update:SessionUpdate} OR {kind:'stop', response:PromptResponse, stopReason:StopReason}. Loop until kind==='stop' to detect turn completion. This is the primary subscribe mechanism for agent_message_chunk/tool_call/tool_call_update/plan/usage_update.

apiSurface.name: ActiveSession.readText / sessionId / modes / newSessionResponse / dispose
apiSurface.signature: get sessionId(): SessionId; get modes(): SessionModeState|null|undefined; get newSessionResponse(): NewSessionResponse; readText(): Promise<string>; dispose(): void; [Symbol.dispose]()
apiSurface.notes: readText() only concatenates agent_message_chunk text and ignores tool_calls/plans. dispose() stops update routing but does NOT close the protocol session. modes/configOptions live on newSessionResponse.

apiSurface.name: ActiveSessionMessage
apiSurface.signature: {kind:'session_update', notification: SessionNotification, update: SessionUpdate} | {kind:'stop', response: PromptResponse, stopReason: StopReason}
apiSurface.notes: Discriminated union returned by nextUpdate().

apiSurface.name: PromptRequest
apiSurface.signature: { sessionId: SessionId; prompt: Array<ContentBlock>; _meta?: {[k:string]:unknown}|null }
apiSurface.notes: VERIFIED at exactly 5017 (matches README). Only sessionId + prompt + _meta. No outputSchema/responseFormat. Structured-output requests must ride in _meta or be encoded into prompt text.

apiSurface.name: PromptResponse
apiSurface.signature: { stopReason: StopReason; usage?: Usage|null; _meta?: {[k:string]:unknown}|null }
apiSurface.notes: VERIFIED at exactly 2943 (matches README). usage is UNSTABLE/experimental. No text/content field — the assistant text arrives only via session/update agent_message_chunk; the response itself carries only stopReason(+usage). Structured result must be read from _meta or reconstructed from streamed chunks.

apiSurface.name: StopReason
apiSurface.signature: "end_turn" | "max_tokens" | "max_turn_requests" | "refusal" | "cancelled"
apiSurface.notes: Full enum. 'cancelled' is the value the agent returns after a session/cancel.

apiSurface.name: Usage
apiSurface.signature: { totalTokens:number; inputTokens:number; outputTokens:number; thoughtTokens?:number|null; cachedReadTokens?:number|null; cachedWriteTokens?:number|null; _meta?:... }
apiSurface.notes: UNSTABLE/experimental. Distinct from UsageUpdate (context-window/cost, types.gen.d.ts:3894) which streams via session/update usage_update with optional Cost{amount,currency} (3871).

apiSurface.name: NewSessionRequest
apiSurface.signature: { cwd: string; additionalDirectories?: Array<string>; mcpServers: Array<McpServer>; _meta?: {[k:string]:unknown}|null }
apiSurface.notes: cwd MUST be absolute. mcpServers is REQUIRED (pass []). NO configOptions field here (README task description was wrong). McpServer union (4605) = http|sse|acp|stdio; McpServerStdio (4727) = {name,command,args,env:EnvVariable[]}.

apiSurface.name: NewSessionResponse
apiSurface.signature: { sessionId: SessionId; modes?: SessionModeState|null; configOptions?: Array<SessionConfigOption>|null; _meta?:... }
apiSurface.notes: configOptions are RETURNED here (and mutated later via session/set_config_option -> SetSessionConfigOptionRequest at 4979), not sent in the request.

apiSurface.name: InitializeRequest / InitializeResponse
apiSurface.signature: req {protocolVersion: ProtocolVersion; clientCapabilities?: ClientCapabilities; clientInfo?: Implementation|null; _meta?} ; resp {protocolVersion; agentCapabilities?: AgentCapabilities; authMethods?: AuthMethod[]; agentInfo?; _meta?}
apiSurface.notes: Send protocolVersion: PROTOCOL_VERSION (=1). ClientCapabilities (4049): fs?{readTextFile,writeTextFile}, terminal?:boolean, session?, etc. AgentCapabilities (1427): loadSession?, promptCapabilities?(1505: image/audio/embeddedContext), mcpCapabilities?, sessionCapabilities?, providers?, nes?. Agent must reply with a version the client supports or client disconnects.

apiSurface.name: SessionNotification / SessionUpdate
apiSurface.signature: SessionNotification {sessionId, update: SessionUpdate, _meta?}; SessionUpdate is a discriminated union on `sessionUpdate`
apiSurface.notes: Variants: user_message_chunk, agent_message_chunk, agent_thought_chunk (all ContentChunk{content:ContentBlock,messageId?}), tool_call (ToolCall), tool_call_update (ToolCallUpdate), plan (Plan), plan_update, plan_removed, available_commands_update, current_mode_update, config_option_update, session_info_update, usage_update (UsageUpdate). Discriminant key is `sessionUpdate`.

apiSurface.name: ToolCall / ToolCallUpdate / ToolCallContent
apiSurface.signature: ToolCall {toolCallId, title, kind?:ToolKind, status?:ToolCallStatus, content?:ToolCallContent[], locations?, rawInput?:unknown, rawOutput?:unknown, _meta?}; ToolCallContent = (Content&{type:'content'})|(Diff&{type:'diff'})|(Terminal&{type:'terminal'})
apiSurface.notes: ToolCallContent VERIFIED at exactly 213 (matches README). ToolKind enum (196) read|edit|delete|move|search|execute|think|fetch|switch_mode|other; ToolCallStatus (204) pending|in_progress|completed|failed. rawInput/rawOutput are typed `unknown` — NO schema validation in core.

apiSurface.name: RequestPermissionRequest
apiSurface.signature: { sessionId: SessionId; toolCall: ToolCallUpdate; options: Array<PermissionOption>; _meta? }
apiSurface.notes: Sent BY agent TO client. We answer by returning a RequestPermissionResponse.

apiSurface.name: RequestPermissionResponse / RequestPermissionOutcome / SelectedPermissionOutcome
apiSurface.signature: resp {outcome: RequestPermissionOutcome, _meta?}; RequestPermissionOutcome = {outcome:'cancelled'} | (SelectedPermissionOutcome & {outcome:'selected'}); SelectedPermissionOutcome {optionId: PermissionOptionId, _meta?}
apiSurface.notes: To allow/deny you return {outcome:{outcome:'selected', optionId}} where optionId is chosen from request.options[]. You select by optionId, NOT by kind.

apiSurface.name: PermissionOption / PermissionOptionKind
apiSurface.signature: PermissionOption {optionId: PermissionOptionId, name: string, kind: PermissionOptionKind, _meta?}; PermissionOptionKind = "allow_once" | "allow_always" | "reject_once" | "reject_always"
apiSurface.notes: kind is a HINT for UI; the actual answer is the optionId. To auto-approve, scan options for kind==='allow_always'/'allow_once' and return that option's optionId.

apiSurface.name: CancelNotification
apiSurface.signature: { sessionId: SessionId; _meta?: {[k:string]:unknown}|null }
apiSurface.notes: Client->agent notification 'session/cancel' (whole prompt turn). Distinct from per-request SendRequestOptions.cancellationSignal which emits '$/cancel_request' for ONE rpc. Agent should finish pending session/update then return PromptResponse{stopReason:'cancelled'}.

apiSurface.name: AGENT_METHODS / CLIENT_METHODS / PROTOCOL_METHODS / PROTOCOL_VERSION
apiSurface.signature: AGENT_METHODS.{initialize,session_new:'session/new',session_prompt:'session/prompt',session_cancel:'session/cancel',session_set_config_option,...}; CLIENT_METHODS.{session_request_permission:'session/request_permission',session_update:'session/update',fs_*,terminal_*,elicitation_*}; PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1
apiSurface.notes: Also exposed ergonomically as `methods` const in acp.d.ts:16-78 (methods.agent.session.prompt etc.). Use these literals for typed onRequest/onNotification/request/notify.

apiSurface.signature: new ClientSideConnection(toClient: (agent: Agent) => Client, stream: Stream) implements Agent; .initialize(p):Promise<InitializeResponse>; .newSession(p):Promise<NewSessionResponse>; .prompt(p):Promise<PromptResponse>; .cancel(p):Promise<void>; .setSessionConfigOption(p); get signal; get closed
apiSurface.notes: Alternative explicit-method client. @deprecated in favor of client()/connectWith. Constructor toClient returns a Client impl (sessionUpdate, requestPermission, readTextFile?, writeTextFile?, createTerminal?...). Useful if the seam prefers imperative agent.prompt() over the ActiveSession pump.

findings.portOrUsageNotes: As CLIENT, the orchestrator drives the agent like this (no Pi coupling anywhere in this SDK — it is provider-agnostic): (1) `import * as acp from '@agentclientprotocol/sdk'`. (2) spawn the agent subprocess with stdio ['pipe','pipe','inherit']. (3) `const stream = acp.ndJsonStream(Writable.toWeb(child.stdin), Readable.toWeb(child.stdout))` — NOTE arg order is (writable-to-agent, readable-from-agent); the example var names are misleading (`input`=writable, `output`=readable). (4) Build the app and register the handlers the agent will call back on US: at minimum `.onRequest(acp.methods.client.session.requestPermission, ...)` returning RequestPermissionResponse, plus optional `fs/read_text_file`, `fs/write_text_file`, `terminal/*`. (5) `.connectWith(stream, async (ctx) => { ... })`. (6) Inside: `await ctx.request(acp.methods.agent.initialize, { protocolVersion: acp.PROTOCOL_VERSION, clientCapabilities: {...} })`. (7) Start a session: `ctx.buildSession({ cwd, mcpServers: [...], _meta })` OR `ctx.buildSession(cwd).withMcpServer(server)`, then `.start()` (or `.withSession(op)` for auto-dispose). cwd MUST be absolute; mcpServers is required (use []). configOptions are NOT settable at session/new — read them from NewSessionResponse.configOptions and mutate via `ctx.request('session/set_config_option', SetSessionConfigOptionRequest)`. (8) Drive a turn: `session.prompt(promptString|ContentBlock[])` then loop `const m = await session.nextUpdate()` switching on `m.kind`: while `'session_update'` route `m.update.sessionUpdate` (agent_message_chunk text, tool_call, tool_call_update, plan, usage_update); on `'stop'` you have `m.response: PromptResponse` -> { stopReason, usage? }. (9) Cancel: whole turn via `ctx.notify(acp.methods.agent.session.cancel, { sessionId })`; single rpc via SendRequestOptions.cancellationSignal (emits $/cancel_request). (10) Lifecycle: connectWith closes on op resolve/reject; ClientConnection exposes .signal/.closed/.close(). ALTERNATIVE imperative client: `new acp.ClientSideConnection(agent => myClientImpl, stream)` gives explicit `.initialize()/.newSession()/.prompt()/.cancel()` (deprecated but simplest to map onto AgentRunner.run). Permissions: to auto-approve, scan RequestPermissionRequest.options for kind 'allow_always'/'allow_once' and return {outcome:{outcome:'selected', optionId}}; to deny, pick 'reject_*' optionId or return {outcome:{outcome:'cancelled'}}. STRUCTURED OUTPUT: not available in core (see seamFacts) — layer it on via _meta or an MCP tool.
findings.seamFacts:
CONFIRMED ZERO native structured output in ACP core: `grep -ciE 'outputSchema|structuredContent|json_schema'` over dist/schema/types.gen.d.ts = 0; case-insensitive `outputSchema|structuredContent|json_schema|jsonSchema|responseSchema|response_format|responseFormat` across ALL dist/*.d.ts = 0 matches. Therefore the AgentRunner seam CANNOT rely on the agent to return validated JSON. Reachability of the 5 cross-cutting concerns the seam must expose:
- SCHEMA/structured result: NOT in core. Must travel via the `_meta?: {[key:string]:unknown}|null` extension point present on EVERY request/response/notification (PromptRequest._meta to ask, PromptResponse._meta / SessionNotification._meta to receive), OR via an MCP workflow tool the agent calls (mcpServers passed at session/new), OR by post-hoc parsing the concatenated agent_message_chunk text. ToolCall.rawInput/rawOutput are typed `unknown` (no validation). So `run(prompt, opts)` result type should be { stopReason: StopReason; usage?: Usage; text?: string (assembled from chunks); meta?: Record<string,unknown>; raw: PromptResponse } and any structured payload is parsed by the workflow layer, not ACP.
- MODEL selection: NOT a first-class field anywhere (no model/modelId in InitializeRequest, NewSessionRequest, or PromptRequest). Reachable only via session config options (SessionConfigOption returned by NewSessionResponse.configOptions, set with session/set_config_option) or agent-specific _meta. Seam `opts` must treat model as agent-specific config, not a portable field.
- PERMISSIONS: first-class. Agent->client request 'session/request_permission' {toolCall, options: PermissionOption[]}; client answers RequestPermissionResponse {outcome: {outcome:'selected', optionId} | {outcome:'cancelled'}}. Kinds allow_once|allow_always|reject_once|reject_always are UI hints; the wire answer is an optionId. The seam should expose a permission policy callback: (req: RequestPermissionRequest) => optionId|'cancel'.
- USAGE: dual + experimental. Final-turn totals on PromptResponse.usage (Usage{totalTokens,inputTokens,outputTokens,thoughtTokens?,cachedReadTokens?,cachedWriteTokens?}) — UNSTABLE. Streaming context/cost via session/update 'usage_update' (UsageUpdate{used,size,cost?:Cost{amount,currency}}). Both optional; the seam must tolerate usage===undefined.
- CANCEL: two mechanisms. (a) whole prompt turn: client notify 'session/cancel' (CancelNotification{sessionId}); agent finishes pending updates then returns PromptResponse{stopReason:'cancelled'}. (b) single rpc: SendRequestOptions.cancellationSignal -> '$/cancel_request'. AgentRunner.run should accept an AbortSignal and map it to session/cancel (turn-level) — the cleaner choice for run() semantics.
ADDITIONAL seam constraints: PROTOCOL_VERSION === 1 (number). Two client styles coexist — fluent `client().connectWith` + ActiveSession (preferred) vs deprecated `ClientSideConnection` (imperative methods). The agent text is ONLY in streamed agent_message_chunk updates; PromptResponse has NO content field, so run() MUST pump session/update to capture output. Transport is stdio newline-delimited JSON via ndJsonStream over Web streams adapted from node:stream; no Pi/runtime-specific dependency.
discrepancies: README task said connection.d.ts holds 'the Connection/Client/Agent classes/interfaces' — FALSE. dist/connection.d.ts is the HTTP/multiplexing layer only (OutboundStream, ConnectionState, ConnectionRegistry, AgentConnector). The actual Client/Agent interfaces and ClientSideConnection/AgentSideConnection classes live in dist/acp.d.ts (Client iface 1322-1462, Agent iface 1469-1774, ClientSideConnection 939-1314).

README task said 'session/new (cwd, mcpServers, configOptions)' — FALSE for configOptions. NewSessionRequest (types.gen.d.ts:4569) has ONLY { cwd, additionalDirectories?, mcpServers, _meta? }. configOptions are RETURNED in NewSessionResponse (2497) and changed via session/set_config_option (SetSessionConfigOptionRequest, 4979). SessionBuilder has no withConfigOption.
Line numbers cited in the prompt are ACCURATE (no drift): PromptRequest at 5017 (exact), PromptResponse at 2943 (exact), ToolCallContent at 213 (exact). All three verified.

Prompt asked whether subscribing to session/update 'is a callback interface the client implements' — BOTH are true: (a) register a notification callback `client().onNotification('session/update', handler)` where handler.params is SessionNotification (or implement Client.sessionUpdate in the legacy path), AND (b) the higher-level ActiveSession.nextUpdate() pump that also surfaces the final PromptResponse as {kind:'stop'}. The canonical example uses the ActiveSession pump and does NOT register an onNotification('session/update') handler.

node-adapter.d.ts is NOT stdio glue (the name might suggest it); it is HTTP/WebSocket server helpers. Stdio is done with node:stream Writable.toWeb/Readable.toWeb + ndJsonStream, per dist/examples/client.js and agent.js.

ndJsonStream parameter order subtlety: declared as ndJsonStream(output: WritableStream, input: ReadableStream) but the client example passes variables named `input`(=Writable.toWeb(stdin)) then `output`(=Readable.toWeb(stdout)); positionally correct (writable first) but the local names are inverted vs the param names — easy to mis-port.
openQuestions: Does registering BOTH an app-level onNotification('session/update') handler AND using ActiveSession double-deliver updates for the same sessionId? The .d.ts doesn't say; ActiveSession keeps a private `registrations` field and routes by sessionId. Needs a quick look at acp.js (impl) before Phase 1 freezes the subscribe model — recommend picking ONE mechanism (ActiveSession pump) for the seam.

Exact mapping of session/prompt errors (auth_required, RequestError codes) to run() rejections — RequestError has static authRequired()/requestCancelled() (jsonrpc.d.ts:494+) but the precise codes/handling for newSession 'auth_required' path were not fully read.

Whether `usage` on PromptResponse is populated by real agents (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, codex) given it is marked UNSTABLE/experimental — affects whether the seam can promise usage. Verify against the actual agent adapters in sibling Phase-0 surfaces.

How configOptions/model are surfaced by concrete agents (claude-agent-acp, codex-acp) — needs the agent-side surfaces to define a portable `opts.model`/`opts.config` mapping; not answerable from this SDK alone.

findings.portOrUsageNotes: As a CLIENT driving this adapter: (1) IN/outputFormat: pass it inside session/new _meta.claudeCode.options.outputFormat = {type:'json_schema', schema}. It is captured at acp-agent.ts:3180 and spread (3220) into the SDK Options literal; none of the ACP-controlled overrides touch outputFormat, so it reaches query() (3347). The SDK Query interface has NO setOutputFormat method (grep found outputFormat only at Options 1645/1651) -> outputFormat is INIT-ONLY; to change schema you must create a new session/query. (2) OUT/structured_output (the rough edge): the adapter NEVER reads SDKResultSuccess.structured_output. The success-subtype handler (1704-1738) only forwards message.result text. To get structured output the client MUST set _meta.claudeCode.emitRawSDKMessages (true, or a filter [{type:'result',subtype:'success'}]) and then listen for the ext notification method '_claude/sdkMessage' whose params = { sessionId, message }, where message is the verbatim SDK message; read message.structured_output off the message where message.type==='result' && message.subtype==='success'. The normal ACP turn-completion path gives you no structured payload. (3) Model: per-call model via _meta.claudeCode.options.model rides the spread into Options.model. Independently, getAvailableModels resolves precedence ANTHROPIC_MODEL -> settings.model -> models[0] and may call query.setModel (3994-4024) ONLY when resolvedFromInput is set and differs/aliased; if neither env nor settings model is set (resolvedFromInput undefined) it skips setModel, leaving the SDK on options.model (i.e. the caller's userProvidedOptions.model). Aliases (opus/sonnet/opusplan->opus, 'opus[1m]' contextHint, family-version compatibility) resolved by resolveModelPreference (3859). Mid-session model change via setSessionConfigOption configId='model' -> query.setModel (2452). (4) permissionMode: from settings.permissions.defaultMode via resolvePermissionMode (570) using PERMISSION_MODE_ALIASES (auto/default/acceptedits->acceptEdits/dontask->dontAsk/plan/bypass(permissions)->bypassPermissions). bypassPermissions gated by ALLOW_BYPASS=!IS_ROOT||IS_SANDBOX (473). Mid-session via setSessionConfigOption configId='mode' -> applySessionMode -> query.setPermissionMode (2485). (5) mcpServers: ACP request.mcpServers normalized into a record (stdio/http/sse, 3127-3151) and merged OVER userProvidedOptions.mcpServers at 3242 ({...userProvidedOptions?.mcpServers, ...mcpServers}) so ACP-supplied servers win on name collision.
findings.seamFacts: For AgentRunner.run(prompt,opts)->result: (A) `opts` maps cleanly onto the SDK Options subset captured as userProvidedOptions; the existing adapter treats outputFormat, model, mcpServers, hooks, tools, env, extraArgs, disallowedTools, agent, settings, additionalDirectories, abortController as caller-controllable, while it FORCES cwd, includePartialMessages, allowDangerouslySkipPermissions, permissionMode, canUseTool, pathToClaudeCodeExecutable, systemPrompt(preset), settingSources, and merges/appends mcpServers/hooks/disallowedTools/extraArgs/env (see NewSessionMeta doc 334-348 + assembly 3216-3324). (B) `result` should expose structured_output, but in this reference it is ONLY reachable as SDKResultSuccess.structured_output (sdk.d.ts:3983) on the type:'result',subtype:'success' message; the adapter drops it (reads message.result string instead). A faithful AgentRunner must capture the terminal SDKResultSuccess message itself, not the adapter's ACP turn result. (C) Terminal/error taxonomy is SDKResultMessage = SDKResultSuccess | SDKResultError; SDKResultError.subtype includes error_max_structured_output_retries (the structured-output retry-exhaustion terminal) alongside error_during_execution/error_max_turns/error_max_budget_usd, each carrying errors:string[]. (D) outputFormat is init-time only (no Query.setOutputFormat) -> run() cannot change schema mid-call; a new query per schema. model and permissionMode ARE mutable mid-session via Query.setModel/setPermissionMode (streaming-input mode only). (E) Raw side-channel contract: method string '_claude/sdkMessage', params {sessionId: string, message: <raw SDKMessage>}; gating via boolean|SDKMessageFilter[] where SDKMessageFilter={type, subtype?, origin?} matched by AND of present fields (shouldEmitRawMessage 3509-3521).
discrepancies: No drift found: every README-cited line is EXACT in this checkout. Verified: userProvidedOptions @3180 (cited ~3180); Options assembly @3216 (~3216); spread ...userProvidedOptions @3220 (~3220); mcpServers merge @3242 (~3242); tools default also @3242-region; Options.outputFormat @1651 (~1651); OutputFormat @1981 (~1981); JsonSchemaOutputFormat @870 (~870); OutputFormatType @1983 (~1983); SDKResultError.subtype with error_max_structured_output_retries @3943 (~3943); adapter handling @1763 (~1763); SDKResultSuccess.structured_output @3983 (~3983); emitRawSDKMessages NewSessionMeta field @357 (~357); wired onto Session @3488 (~3488); raw-forward emit guard @1337 / actual extNotification call @1341 (cited ~1337).
README implied 'the file is far longer than citations implied' but the cited line numbers are accurate for the b8df8e0e checkout; acp-agent.ts is 4825 lines total.

Nuance not in README: the adapter NEVER reads structured_output. The success-subtype handler (1704-1738) consumes only message.result. structured_output is exclusively surfaced via the raw _claude/sdkMessage notification. This is the central rough edge for the rebuild.
Nuance: emitRawSDKMessages appears at THREE places - Session field decl @261, NewSessionMeta type field @357, and Session construction @3488. README cited only ~357.
openQuestions: The SDK has no setOutputFormat on Query (confirmed by grep: outputFormat only at Options 1645/1651). Confirm the rebuild accepts per-session (not per-prompt) outputFormat, or whether AgentRunner.run must spawn a fresh query when opts.outputFormat changes between calls.

getAvailableModels resolves model from ANTHROPIC_MODEL/settings.model only, NOT from userProvidedOptions.model; userProvidedOptions.model still flows via the Options spread. Confirm intended precedence when a caller passes opts.model AND ANTHROPIC_MODEL is set (env wins via setModel override, potentially overriding opts.model).

structured_output is typed `unknown` on SDKResultSuccess; the schema-conformance/validation is the SDK's responsibility (error_max_structured_output_retries is the failure terminal). Confirm whether the rebuilt result type should re-validate against the JSON schema or trust the SDK.

Raw notification forwards the whole SDKMessage including potentially large/streamed content; for the new mcp-server seam, confirm whether to forward only result-subtype messages (a tight {type:'result',subtype:'success'} filter) to avoid flooding the workflow-engine.

findings.source: codex-acp (Codex ACP adapter that bridges ACP <-> Codex app-server JSON-RPC). Read on disk at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-vikash-agentprism-workflows/457dd4e8-a7d6-4cb8-bc8a-608bdf0c3cfd/scratchpad/phase0/sources/codex-acp
findings.purpose: This is the CODEX structured-output + model path: the reference adapter showing how an ACP agent server is driven on top of a per-turn JSON-RPC app-server. For the new 3-module system it is the canonical example of (a) the AgentRunner seam shape -- one in-flight prompt per session, generation/abort fencing, model+effort selection, per-turn outputSchema capability; and (b) the structured-output contract: turn-level outputSchema (constrain final assistant message) vs tool-level outputSchema/structuredContent (MCP). It is the model the acp-agents module's CodexAgentRunner should mirror.

keyFiles.role: v2 app-server turn params. This is the path the server ACTUALLY uses (method 'turn/start'). outputSchema?: JsonValue|null constrains the final assistant message per turn; model is a plain string (base model id, NOT bracketed); effort is a separate ReasoningEffort field.
v1 app-server turn params (older API). Also carries outputSchema: JsonValue|null. EXPORTED (app-server/index.ts:253) but NEVER imported/used by the implementation -- the server speaks v2 only.

Typed shape of a tool's output schema {properties?, required?, type}.
MCP tool result carrying structuredContent?: JsonValue.
v2 MCP tool call result: structuredContent: JsonValue|null (required key, nullable).
v2 MCP server tool call response: structuredContent?: JsonValue (optional).
JsonValue union used by every schema/structured field.
The orchestrating ACP server. SessionState interface, the private sessions Map, generation/close fencing maps, and the prompt()/cancel() lifecycle = the AgentRunner seam reference.
Builds the turn (sendPrompt -> runTurn) and the MCP server config (createMcpSeverConfig: stdio command-based + http; rejects acp/sse). sendPrompt sets model+effort but does NOT set outputSchema.
JSON-RPC client to Codex app-server. Imports TurnStartParams from ./app-server/v2; turnStart() calls method 'turn/start'; runTurn() awaits turn/completed.

Builds the ACP session config 'model' and 'reasoning_effort' SELECT options (separate selects).

Entrypoint wiring ACP methods -> CodexAcpServer (session.prompt->prompt, session.cancel->cancel, session.setConfigOption->setSessionConfigOption).

apiSurface.name: promptShouldStop
apiSurface.signature: private promptShouldStop(sessionId: string, activePrompt: ActivePrompt): boolean { return activePrompt.signal.aborted || this.activePrompts.get(sessionId) !== activePrompt || this.sessionIsClosing(sessionId); }
apiSurface.notes: The generation-fence check: a turn is stale if its prompt was aborted, superseded by a newer ActivePrompt for the same session, or the session is closing. Late-started turns are interrupted via interruptLateStartedTurn.

findings.portOrUsageNotes: ENGINE / SEAM (acp-agents CodexAgentRunner): mirror CodexAcpServer's per-session concurrency model, not a generic queue. Exact Pi-/Codex-coupling points to lift or re-implement: (1) sessions: Map<string,SessionState> (CodexAcpServer.ts:142) plus the sibling maps activePrompts/pendingTurnStarts/closingSessions/sessionGenerations/sessionOpenGenerations (143-148) -- the AgentRunner needs at most ONE in-flight prompt per session; a second run() supersedes the first (promptShouldStop, 1330) and the stale turn is turn/interrupt-ed. (2) run(prompt,opts) maps to CodexAcpServer.prompt (1401): it creates an ActivePrompt (AbortSignal + closeSignal + cancelSignal, 1188-1239), subscribes event/approval/elicitation handlers, races sendPrompt vs closeSignal vs cancelBeforeTurnStarted, and returns {stopReason,'end_turn'|cancelled, usage, _meta}. cancel(opts) maps to CodexAcpServer.cancel (1646) -> turn/interrupt -> Codex emits turn/completed status 'interrupted'. (3) The Codex app-server is reached over JSON-RPC v2 (method 'turn/start', CodexAppServerClient.ts:240) with TurnStartParams: model is the BASE id string and effort is a SEPARATE ReasoningEffort -- the 'model[effort]' encoding (ModelId, ModelId.ts) is purely an ACP-surface convention that is split apart before hitting the app-server. (4) outputSchema: the v2 TurnStartParams field EXISTS (TurnStartParams.ts:45) but the adapter NEVER populates it (sendPrompt, CodexAcpClient.ts:584-593, omits it), and ACP PromptRequest has no outputSchema field -- so in THIS build a client CANNOT set per-turn structured output through codex-acp. To add it to the new AgentRunner you must thread an opts.outputSchema (JsonValue) from run() down into the runTurn(...) object as outputSchema. The ONLY client-controllable structured-output surface today is MCP tool-level: Tool.outputSchema (Tool.ts:9) + result structuredContent (CallToolResult.ts:9 / v2 McpToolCallResult.ts:6 / v2 McpServerToolCallResponse.ts:6). USAGE (mcp-server / workflow-engine as client): drive sessions via ACP methods (index.ts:106-133): session.new/load/resume, session.setConfigOption (configId 'model' / 'reasoning_effort', value is a plain string -- see ModelConfigOption.ts), session.prompt, session.cancel. Client-provided MCP servers must be stdio (command/args/env) or http (url/headers); acp and sse are hard-rejected (CodexAcpClient.ts:460-463) and the agent advertises mcpCapabilities {acp:false, http:true, sse:false} (CodexAcpServer.ts:209-213).
findings.seamFacts: Constraints Phase 1 should bake into the AgentRunner interface + shared types: (1) CONCURRENCY: one active prompt per session; run() must supersede/abort any prior in-flight run for the same session (model: ActivePrompt + activePrompts Map + promptShouldStop fence). result carries stopReason ('end_turn' | cancelled/interrupted), usage, and a _meta blob. (2) CANCELLATION is first-class: run() takes an AbortSignal; cancel maps to an interrupt RPC and resolves the turn as 'interrupted', not an error. (3) MODEL+EFFORT are two distinct knobs at the wire level even though ACP encodes them as one 'model[effort]' string; shared types should keep model:string and effort:ReasoningEffort|null separate and treat the bracket encoding as a display/config-id convention only. (4) STRUCTURED OUTPUT has TWO independent levels that must be distinct types: turn-level outputSchema: JsonValue|null (constrains the FINAL assistant message; belongs on the turn/run options) vs tool-level Tool.outputSchema + result structuredContent (MCP tool I/O). Do not conflate them. JsonValue = number|string|boolean|array|object|null is the schema/structured carrier type. (5) The reference adapter does NOT currently expose turn-level outputSchema to clients -- if the new system wants 'constrain final message', the AgentRunner run() options must add outputSchema and the runner must forward it into turn/start (a net-new wire param, the field already exists in TurnStartParams). (6) MCP transport whitelist for the mcp-server module: stdio + http only; reject acp and sse. (7) The server is v2-only (method 'turn/start'); v1 SendUserTurnParams is dead code -- Phase 1 should target the v2 shapes.

findings.source: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk — TypeScript MCP SDK (stdio server side that will host the `workflow` tool)
findings.purpose: This is the mcp-server module's SHELL. It constructs an McpServer, registers a single `workflow` tool whose Zod inputSchema is the run envelope (script/args/maxAgents/concurrency/agentRetries/agentTimeoutMs/tokenBudget/resumeFromRunId), connects over StdioServerTransport, runs the workflow-engine SYNCHRONOUSLY inside the tool handler, streams mid-call progress via notifications/progress, returns a CallToolResult, and (optionally) drives checkpoint() via server.elicitInput. It is a pure transport/protocol adapter that calls into workflow-engine; it does NOT itself touch the AgentRunner seam.
